# Doctors360
> Nonprofit healthcare clinic and registered NGO based in Juba, South Sudan. Providing accessible, patient-centred healthcare across East Africa since 2019.

## About
Doctors360 is an integrated medical services provider and registered NGO committed to making quality, ethical, patient-centred healthcare accessible to all — digitally and physically, across Africa. Founded in 2019 in Juba, South Sudan, Doctors360 has served over 50,000 patients across South Sudan and Uganda.

- Website: https://www.doctors360.org
- Email: care@doctors360.com
- Phone: +211 927 702 808
- Location: Juba, South Sudan (4.859363, 31.57125)
- Hours: Monday–Saturday, 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM

## Mission & Vision
- Mission: To provide accessible, trusted, and patient-centered healthcare digitally and physically across Africa.
- Vision: A healthy nation where quality healthcare is within reach for everyone.
- Purpose: To make quality, ethical, and patient-centred healthcare accessible through trusted medical professionals and innovative digital solutions.
- Slogan: The Choice of a Healthy Nation

## Pages
- [Home](https://www.doctors360.org/): Main landing page with overview of services, departments, testimonials, and appointment booking.
- [About Us](https://www.doctors360.org/about): Organization history, leadership team, governance board, strategic objectives, and timeline.
- [NGO Programs](https://www.doctors360.org/programs): Community health programs, impact statistics, partnerships, and geographic reach across East Africa.
- [News & Blog](https://www.doctors360.org/news): Medical articles, community stories, organisational updates, and health education.
- [Gallery](https://www.doctors360.org/gallery): Photos from community outreach, medical centre, and events.
- [Donate & Support](https://www.doctors360.org/donate): Donation tiers, volunteer opportunities, corporate partnerships, and support form.

## Medical Departments (12 Specialties)
- General Medicine — Comprehensive primary care for all ages
- Emergency Care — 24/7 rapid-response emergency services
- Cardiology — Heart care from prevention to intervention
- Pediatrics — Evidence-based care for children and adolescents
- Mental Health — Counselling, therapy, and psychiatric support
- Orthopedics — Bone, joint, and musculoskeletal care
- Ophthalmology — Full-spectrum eye care
- Obstetrics & Gynecology — Maternal and reproductive health
- Radiology & Imaging — Digital X-ray, ultrasound, and advanced imaging
- Laboratory Services — Diagnostic laboratory for blood, urine, and pathology
- Pharmacy Services — Prescription and over-the-counter medications
- Dermatology — Skin, hair, and nail care for tropical climates

## Four-Pillar Care Model
1. Prevention & Wellness — Check-ups, vaccinations, lifestyle counseling, and preventive screenings
2. Advanced Diagnostics — Imaging and lab services for fast, accurate results
3. Treatment & Therapy — Personalized care plans by multidisciplinary teams
4. Follow-up & Recovery — Post-treatment check-ins, progress monitoring, and referrals

## Key Statistics
- 50,000+ patients served since 2019
- 200+ community health workers trained
- 12,000+ outreach visits per year
- 5 communities reached
- 30% of care provided free of charge
- 8 years in operation
- 6 counties active
- 3 countries reached (South Sudan, Uganda, expanding)
- 8 active partnerships

## Leadership
- Tenywa Daniel — Founder & Team Lead
- Funankya Joseph — Co-Founder & Medical Director
- Niinsima Cathy — Partnerships & Programs Director

## Board of Directors
- Hon. Peter Kolong — Board Chair
- Dr. Sarah Machar — Vice Chair
- Beatrice Lam — Board Secretary
- Simon Taban — Treasurer
- Prof. David Okello — Independent Member
- Dr. Fatima Al-Hassan — Independent Member

## Strategic Objectives (2025–2027)
1. Expand Access Across Africa — Establish facilities and digital health services in underserved communities
2. Health Education at Scale — Train 500+ community health workers annually
3. Sustainable Healthcare Delivery — Build financially sustainable models
4. Digital Health Integration — Launch patient portal, telemedicine, and lab results platform

## Partners
- Ministry of Health, South Sudan
- World Health Organization (WHO)
- UNICEF South Sudan
- MSF (Médecins Sans Frontières)
- African Development Bank
- German Development Cooperation (GIZ)

## Content Categories
- Community Health
- Maternal Health
- Mental Health
- Child Health
- Organisational Update
- Community Stories

## Ways to Support
- Donate ($25 Community Supporter / $100 Health Champion / $500 Program Partner)
- Volunteer (Medical, Training, Digital/Tech, Communications, Fundraising, Research)
- Corporate Partnership (cause marketing, employee volunteering, in-kind donations, sponsorship)
